ddls:
CREATE TABLE "locations" (
  "location_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each location */,
  "location_name" TEXT /* Name of the location */,
  "country" TEXT /* Country where the location is situated */,
  "description" TEXT /* Brief description of the location */,
  "latitude" TEXT /* Latitude of the location */,
  "longitude" TEXT /* Longitude of the location */,
  "altitude" TEXT /* Altitude of the location */,
  "time_zone" TEXT /* Time zone of the location */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("location_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "climatic_features" (
  "feature_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each climatic feature */,
  "feature_name" TEXT /* Name of the climatic feature (e.g., Temperature Range, Avg. Temperature) */,
  "unit" TEXT /* Unit of measurement for the climatic feature (e.g., °C, mm) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Brief description of the climatic feature */,
  "min_value" TEXT /* Minimum value for the climatic feature */,
  "max_value" TEXT /* Maximum value for the climatic feature */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("feature_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "location_features" (
  "location_id" INTEGER /* ID of the location */,
  "feature_id" INTEGER /* ID of the climatic feature */,
  "feature_value" TEXT /* Value of the climatic feature for the location */,
  "data_source" TEXT /* Source of the climatic data */,
  "data_date" TEXT /* Date when the climatic data was recorded */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("location_id", "feature_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "reviews" (
  "review_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each review */,
  "location_id" INTEGER /* ID of the location being reviewed */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who wrote the review */,
  "rating" INTEGER /* Rating given by the user (e.g., 1-5) */,
  "review_text" TEXT /* Text of the review */,
  "review_date" TEXT /* Date when the review was written */,
  "helpful_votes" INTEGER /* Number of helpful votes for the review */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("review_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_reviews_location_id FOREIGN KEY ("location_id") REFERENCES locations ("location_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_reviews_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "password" TEXT /* Password for the user (hashed for security) */,
  "registration_date" TEXT /* Date when the user registered */,
  "profile_picture" TEXT /* Profile picture of the user */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "recommendations" (
  "recommendation_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each recommendation */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who received the recommendation */,
  "location_id" INTEGER /* ID of the recommended location */,
  "recommendation_date" TEXT /* Date when the recommendation was made */,
  "reason" TEXT /* Reason for the recommendation */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("recommendation_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_recommendations_location_id FOREIGN KEY ("location_id") REFERENCES locations ("location_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_recommendations_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "location_types" (
  "type_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each location type */,
  "type_name" TEXT /* Name of the location type */,
  "description" TEXT /* Brief description of the location type */,
  "icon" TEXT /* Icon representing the location type */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "location_type_map" (
  "location_id" INTEGER /* ID of the location */,
  "type_id" INTEGER /* ID of the location type */,
  "primary_type" INTEGER /* Whether this is the primary type for the location */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("location_id", "type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_preferences" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user */,
  "type_id" INTEGER /* ID of the location type */,
  "preference_value" INTEGER /* Value of the user's preference for the location type (e.g., 1-5) */,
  "preference_date" TEXT /* Date when the user set their preference */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id", "type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "admins" (
  "admin_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each administrator */,
  "admin_name" TEXT /* Full name of the administrator */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the administrator */,
  "password" TEXT /* Password for the administrator (hashed for security) */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the administrator (e.g., superadmin, location admin) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("admin_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "location_admins" (
  "location_id" INTEGER /* ID of the location */,
  "admin_id" INTEGER /* ID of the administrator responsible for the location */,
  "responsibility" TEXT /* Type of responsibility (e.g., content, moderation) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("location_id", "admin_id")
)
